# LegalEase Landing Site — Developer Handoff

This package contains a complete, working front-end reference for the LegalEase marketing site (the "Open Door" landing page plus four supporting pages) and everything needed to take it to production.

**Read this first.** The HTML files are a high-fidelity *reference implementation*, not a production codebase. They render and behave exactly as intended, but they were generated by a build script with all assets inlined as base64. Before production you will externalize assets, wire the stubbed backends, and reimplement in your stack. This doc tells you exactly what to do and what not to assume.

---

## 1. What's in this package

```
handoff/
  HANDOFF.md                ← this file
  DESIGN-TOKENS.md          ← colors, type, spacing, the full design system
  STUBS-AND-TODOS.md        ← every placeholder/dead link and what to wire
  pages/                    ← the five HTML files (asset-inlined reference)
    legalease-opendoor.html
    waitlist.html
    contact.html
    terms.html
    disclaimer.html
  assets/                   ← externalized, web-optimized source images
    brand/                  ← favicon, OG card, LE mark (PNG + SVG)
    logos/                  ← product + company logos
    investors/              ← the seven "Backed by" fund logos
    wilma/                  ← Wilma mascot poses (used in the cinematic opening)
```

---

## 2. The five pages and how they relate

| File | Purpose | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| `legalease-opendoor.html` | Main landing page | Cinematic scroll opening, product catalog, trust, partners, Wilma chat bubble |
| `waitlist.html` | Roadmap-product signup | Reads `?product=recordshield\|startapart\|claimcoach` to pre-select |
| `contact.html` | Contact + routing | Reads `?topic=support\|partnership\|press\|other` to pre-select |
| `terms.html` | Terms and Conditions | Long-form legal doc, sticky TOC, scroll-spy |
| `disclaimer.html` | Website Disclaimer | Same legal-doc template as Terms |

**Cross-linking:** all five use *relative* links to each other (e.g. `href="contact.html"`, `href="waitlist.html?product=..."`, footer legal links, "back to LegalEase"). They must be served from the **same directory**, OR you must update the links to match your routing. If the landing page becomes `index.html` (or `/`), update every internal reference accordingly. Search each file for `.html"` to find them all.

---

## 3. CRITICAL: assets are inlined — externalize them

Every image in the pages is embedded as a base64 data URI. That's why `legalease-opendoor.html` is ~4.8MB. **Do not ship this as-is.**

For production:
1. Replace each `src="data:image/png;base64,..."` with a real path to the corresponding file in `assets/`.
2. The asset filenames in `assets/` are descriptive; the mapping is documented in section 7 below.
3. Serve images from a CDN / static host; add width/height attributes and `loading="lazy"` on below-the-fold images.
4. The Wilma poses in the cinematic opening are the heaviest images — lazy-load or preload deliberately depending on how you reimplement the scroll sequence.

The single biggest production win is getting the landing page from ~4.8MB inlined down to a small HTML file + cached external assets.

---

## 4. The build system (FYI — you will likely replace it)

The HTML was generated by Python scripts (`build.py`, `build_legal.py`) that inject base64 assets into templates containing `{{ASSET:name}}` placeholders. **You do not need to adopt this pipeline.** Treat the HTML in `pages/` as the design reference and reimplement in your stack (the project's existing stack is Supabase / Vercel / Next per the team's setup).

Important consequence: the HTML files are *outputs*. If anyone edits them directly and the script is re-run, edits are lost. Once you take over, this pipeline is retired — the HTML becomes your source, or you port it into components.

---

## 5. Interactions to reimplement

- **Cinematic scroll opening** (landing): a `position: sticky` stage (~520vh tall) drives a scroll-progress calc that cross-fades Wilma poses and reveals three product "rooms," then a welcome pose. Logic is plain JS in the page. Note: `html { scroll-behavior: smooth }` animates programmatic scrolls — relevant if you write scroll tests (it can produce mid-animation reads).
- **Anchor nav** with `scroll-margin-top: 80px` on targets so sections clear the fixed nav.
- **Wilma chat bubble** (landing): a floating circular launcher → chat panel. It is a **scripted persona preview, not an LLM** (see section 6). All responses are canned; freeform input is routed by simple keyword rules.
- **Waitlist / contact forms**: client-side validation + success state; submission is **stubbed** (see STUBS-AND-TODOS.md).
- **Legal pages**: sticky TOC with `IntersectionObserver` scroll-spy.

---

## 6. The Wilma chat bubble — read before touching

The bubble on the landing page is a **deliberately scripted, model-free persona preview.** It does not call any AI model. Its safety (no legal verdicts, no outcome predictions, routes hard-stops to a human) holds *by construction* because nothing generates text.

**Do not wire this bubble to a live LLM as part of the marketing site.** The real, model-backed Wilma is a separate, much larger engineering effort (content-injection contract, adversarial test gate, telemetry, kill-switch, counsel sign-off) documented in the Wilma safety specs, and it belongs in the consumer product, not the landing page. If you replace the scripted bubble with anything that generates, you take on that entire safety system. Keep the preview scripted.

---

## 7. Asset mapping (inlined name → file)

Logos / brand:
- `logo_legalease_nav` → `assets/logos/legalease-wordmark.png`
- `logo_expungement` → `assets/logos/expungement-ai.png`
- `logo_expungement_icon` → `assets/logos/expungement-ai-icon.png`
- `logo_rcap` → `assets/logos/rcap.png`
- `logo_recordshield_mark` → `assets/logos/record-shield-mark.png`
- `logo_startapart` → `assets/logos/startapart.png`
- `logo_claimcoach` → `assets/logos/claimcoach.png`
- `le_mark` → `assets/brand/le-mark.png` (+ `le-mark.svg`, vector)
- `favicon_32` / `favicon_180` → `assets/brand/favicon-32.png` / `favicon-180.png`
- `og_card` → `assets/brand/og-card.png`

Investors ("Backed by" strip):
- `inv_techstars, inv_slauson, inv_sv2, inv_goldhouse, inv_samvid, inv_copacetic, inv_innovate`
  → `assets/investors/{techstars,slauson,sv2,gold-house,samvid,copacetic,innovate-mississippi}.png`

Wilma poses:
- `wilma_avatar` → `assets/wilma/wilma-avatar.png` (chat panel header)
- `wilma_doors, wilma_push1, wilma_push3, wilma_presenting, wilma_tablet, wilma_welcome, wilma_close, wilma_thumbsup`
  → `assets/wilma/wilma-*.png` (cinematic opening)

**Vector note:** only the LE mark is provided as SVG (reconstructed). The product/company logos are raster only here — if you need them as vectors for crisp scaling, request the originals from whoever produced them. The investor logos were normalized to navy at reduced opacity for the strip; originals belong to those funds.

---

## 8. Production checklist (the actual gap to "live")

Engineering:
- [ ] Externalize all base64 assets → external files + CDN (section 3)
- [ ] Reimplement pages in production stack; retire the Python build (section 4)
- [ ] Wire waitlist form backend (STUBS-AND-TODOS.md #1)
- [ ] Wire contact form backend (STUBS-AND-TODOS.md #2)
- [ ] Host `og-card.png` at a public URL; replace `REPLACE-WITH-YOUR-DOMAIN` in all 5 pages' OG meta (STUBS-AND-TODOS.md #3)
- [ ] Add real social-profile URLs to footer (STUBS-AND-TODOS.md #4)
- [ ] Add analytics at deploy (privacy-first tool recommended; ties to privacy policy)
- [ ] Confirm relative-link strategy matches final routing (section 2)
- [ ] Responsive QA pass (pages are built mobile-responsive; verify in target browsers)

Content / legal (NOT developer tasks — flagged so nothing ships missing them):
- [ ] **Privacy Policy** written and linked — currently a dead `#`, referenced throughout Terms & Disclaimer. Highest-priority gap; product handles criminal-record data.
- [ ] Counsel sign-off on Terms/Disclaimer substance + UPL language
- [ ] Founder quote (Lawrence) approved
- [ ] Testimonial (Ricky W.) confirmed real + cleared for use (FTC endorsement rules)
- [ ] Cookie/consent banner if analytics + applicable jurisdictions

---

## 9. One-paragraph summary for whoever runs the project

The five HTML files in `pages/` are a complete, accurate front-end reference — every screen, state, interaction, and all copy. To productionize: externalize the inlined assets (provided clean in `assets/`), reimplement in your stack, and wire the two stubbed forms plus the OG-image and social URLs. The Wilma chat bubble must stay scripted (not an LLM) on the marketing site. Separately and before launch, the content/legal items — especially the missing Privacy Policy — must be resolved; those are not engineering tasks. See `STUBS-AND-TODOS.md` for the exact, line-locatable list and `DESIGN-TOKENS.md` for the design system.
